A stochastic meta-population model of LSDV epidemics

Thibaut Jombart

  • Outline of the work
  • Simulation setup
    • Required libraries
  • Population setting
  • Seasonality
  • Simulations
    • General settings
    • baseline scenario: no response
    • cull_quarant_vacc scenario: mass culling and ring vaccination
    • quarant_vacc scenario: quarantine and ring vaccination
    • selcull_quarant_vacc scenario: selective culling, quarantine and ring vaccination
    • selcull_quarant_vacc_insect scenario: selective culling, quarantine, ring vaccination, insecticide (all but mass culling)
    • all scenario: mass culling, quarantine, ring vaccination, insecticide
  • Analyses

Outline of the work

In this report, we use a stochastic, meta-population SEIRCDV model to simulate outbreaks of Lumpy skin disease virus. We contrast different response scenario:

  1. baseline: no response
  2. cull_quarant_vacc scenario: mass culling of infected farms, quarantine (local and ring), ring vaccination
  3. quarant_vacc: quarantine (local and ring) and vaccination (local and ring)
  4. selcull_quarant_vacc: selective culling (only individuals in \(I\)), quarantine (local and ring) and vaccination (local and ring)
  5. selcull_quarant_vacc_insect scenario: all but mass culling (selective culling, vaccination, quarantine, insecticide)
  6. all: all measures combined

Simulation setup

Required libraries

We load required libraries:

library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.5
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.1     ✔ tibble    3.3.0
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
## ✔ purrr     1.2.0     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(viridis)
## Loading required package: viridisLite
library(qs)
## qs 0.27.3. Announcement: https://github.com/qsbase/qs/issues/103
library(here)
## here() starts at C:/dev/lsdsim
library(digest)
library(compiler)
library(rio)
library(cowplot)
## 
## Attaching package: 'cowplot'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:lubridate':
## 
##     stamp
devtools::load_all()
## ℹ Loading lsdsim

Population setting

Our benchmark suggests simulations for 365 days become prohibitively slow for grid sizes exceeding 40 (i.e., 1600 farms). For the final simulation sets, we aim for 1000 replicates in each setting, so we will use a grid size of 40. We assume a diffusion across neighbouring farms of 1%, i.e. 1% of transmission moves to a different farm nearby.

n_rep <- params$n_rep
duration <- 365
grid_size <- 40
n_pop <- grid_size^2
xy <- make_grid(grid_size)

The size of the farms, in number of herds, is drawn using an average herd size of 50, exponentially distributed with a minimum at 20 individuals:

r_herd_size <- function(n) {
  20 + round(rexp(nrow(xy), 1/30))
}

herd_size <- r_herd_size(1e4)
hist(herd_size, border = "white", 
     main = "Herd size distribution",
     xlab = "Number of cows", 
     nclass = 30, 
     xlim = c(0, max(herd_size)))

summary(herd_size)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
##   20.00   29.00   41.00   49.99   61.00  284.00
herds <- cbind.data.frame(xy, size = r_herd_size(n_pop))

ggplot(herds, aes(x = x, y = y)) + 
  geom_tile(aes(fill = size)) + 
  theme_bw() + 
  scale_fill_viridis_c("Herd size", n.breaks = 8) + 
  labs(title = "Example of a meta-population")

In practice, every simulation will use a different population setting (randomly drawn herd sizes).

Seasonality

Seasonality is likely to affect transmissibility via changes in vector populations (‘beta’ term in the model). We build a simple function that will create R values seasonal fluctuations using a cosine:

#' param t: a vector of integers representing days
R_season <- function(start = 1, duration = 365, min_R = 2, max_R = 20) {
  t <- seq(start, by = 1, length.out = duration)
  out <- cos(2 * pi * (t)/ 365)
  # rescale to [0 ; 1]
  out <- out - min(out)
  out <- out / max(out)
  
  ## scale to [min_R ; max_R]
  out <- out * (max_R - min_R)
  out <- out + min_R
  out
}

A random starting point will enable simulating seasonality with an outbreak seeded at any point in the year. We illustrate 10 trajectories with a random start:

R_demo <- lapply(1:10, function(i) R_season(
  start = sample(1:365, 1), 
  min_R = runif(1, 0, 2),
  max_R = runif(1, 15, 25))) %>% 
  data.frame()
matplot(R_demo, 
        x = 1:365, 
        xlab = "Time (day since disease introduction)", 
        ylab = "R", type = "l", col = hcl.colors(10), lty = 1, lwd = 2,
        main = "Seasonality in R - examples")

Simulations

General settings

We set the number of replicates, which will apply to each simulation scenario. We also make a compiled version of lsdsim and set up initial conditions.

fast_lsdsim <- compiler::cmpfun(lsdsim)

This function is used to generate one new case in a randomly chosen population, to seed outbreaks:

r_ini_case <- function(n_pop) {
  out <- rep(0, n_pop)
  out[sample.int(n_pop, 1)] <- 1
  out
}

Input parameters will be the same for different interventions. We need to uses the values of R drawn from the literature generated by rlsd_param with values fluctuating over time (seasonality), both for symptomatic and asymptomatic cases.

lsd_params <- rlsd_param(n_rep)
head(lsd_params)
##       R0_I     R0_A   latent infec_period        cfr    pasymp   beta_I
## 1 20.16764 1.740942 7.366904     8.761859 0.03041798 0.5077491 2.301753
## 2 19.79453 1.852755 8.360274     8.748649 0.02465021 0.5408347 2.262581
## 3 20.54244 2.195071 8.210977     9.079335 0.01969693 0.4882512 2.262549
## 4 20.39844 1.841726 8.325875     9.452608 0.02709598 0.5223774 2.157969
## 5 20.25808 1.888889 8.715768     8.768966 0.03371457 0.5342960 2.310202
## 6 19.55189 2.001035 7.697993     8.414090 0.02043976 0.4738647 2.323709
##      beta_A     sigma     gamma
## 1 0.1986955 0.1357422 0.1141310
## 2 0.2117761 0.1196133 0.1143034
## 3 0.2417656 0.1217882 0.1101402
## 4 0.1948379 0.1201075 0.1057909
## 5 0.2154061 0.1147346 0.1140385
## 6 0.2378195 0.1299040 0.1188483

start_dates <- sample.int(365, n_rep, replace = TRUE)
R0_I_season <- lapply(
  seq_len(n_rep),
  function(i) R_season(start = start_dates[i], 
                       max_R = lsd_params$R0_I[i], 
                       min_R = 2)
  )
R0_A_season <- lapply(
  seq_len(n_rep),
  function(i) R_season(start = start_dates[i], 
                       max_R = lsd_params$R0_A[i], 
                       min_R = 0)
  )

## We make a figure keep at most the first 10 simulated time series:
R_over_time <- data.frame(
  Symptomatic = unlist(head(R0_I_season, 10)),
  Asymptomatic = unlist(head(R0_A_season, 10)),
  time = 1:365
)
n_sim_kept <- min(10, n_rep)
R_over_time$sim = rep(1:n_sim_kept, each = 365)

R_over_time %>%
  pivot_longer(1:2, values_to = "R", names_to = "Source") %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = time, y = R)) + 
  geom_line(aes(group = sim, color = factor(sim))) + 
  guides(color = "none") +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "turbo") +
  facet_wrap(~Source, nrow = 2, scales = "free_y") + 
  theme_bw() + 
  labs(x = "Time (days since introduction)", y = "Reproduction number (R0)")

R0 are translated into betas and the rest of the inputs are generated:

beta_I_season <- lapply(
  seq_along(R0_I_season), 
  function(i) R0_I_season[[i]] * lsd_params$gamma[i]
)
beta_A_season <- lapply(
  seq_along(R0_A_season), 
  function(i) R0_A_season[[i]] * lsd_params$gamma[i]
)


list_herd_size <- lapply(1:n_rep, function(i) r_herd_size(n_pop))
list_ini_cases <- lapply(1:n_rep, function(i) r_ini_case(n_pop))

## variability in interventions:
##
## - response time 2-14 days
## - quarantine efficacy within: 0-20%
## - quarantine efficacy outwards: 80-100%
## - insecticide efficacy: 50-95%

inputs <- tibble(
  lsd_params %>% select(-c(beta_I, beta_A)), 
  ini_S = list_herd_size,
  ini_I = list_ini_cases, 
  beta_I = beta_I_season,
  beta_A = beta_A_season,
  interv_delay = sample(2:14, replace = TRUE, size = n_rep),
  quarant_eff_in = runif(n_rep, min = 0, max = 0.2),
  quarant_eff_out = runif(n_rep, min = 0.8, max = 1),
  insect_eff = runif(n_rep, min = 0.5, max = 0.95)
)

inputs$label <- apply(inputs, 1, function(e) substr(digest(e), 1, 8))

We also need to pre-generate delta matrices for each simulation. As they are large, we need to store them as separate files as they would otherwise clog the RAM:

delta_dir <- here::here("outputs", "delta")
dir.create(delta_dir)

for (i in seq_len(n_rep)) {
  ## the  delta is generated on the fly to avoid tremendous RAM requirements; we
  ## proceed as follows:
  ##
  ## generate the delta matrix from the rook connectivity with diffusion ranging
  ## 1-10% from one location to another; there are 1-5% of cliques, which are
  ## connected to 1-3% of all other populations)
  delta <- make_delta(xy, 
                      diffusion = runif(nrow(xy), min = 0.01, max = 0.1), 
                      p_clique = runif(1, min = 0.01, max = 0.05),
                      clique_connect = sample(5:30, 1)
  )
  file_name <- paste0("delta_", i, ".qs")
  qsave(delta, file.path(delta_dir, file_name))
  
}

delta_files <- list.files(delta_dir, full.names = TRUE)

baseline scenario: no response

In these simulations, we use no response.

output_dir <- here::here("outputs", "simulations", "baseline", Sys.Date())
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

res_baseline <- vector(mode = "list", length = n_rep)
for (i in seq_len(n_rep)) {

  ## read a delta file for this simulation
  delta <- qread(delta_files[i])
  
  ## run simulation
  current_sim <- fast_lsdsim(
    grid_size = grid_size, 
    time = duration,
    ini_S = unlist(inputs$ini_S[[i]]), 
    ini_I = unlist(inputs$ini_I[[i]]),
    delta = delta,
    beta_A = inputs$beta_A[[i]],
    beta_I = inputs$beta_I[[i]],
    sigma = inputs$sigma[i],
    gamma = inputs$gamma[i],
    pasymp = inputs$pasymp[i],
    cfr = inputs$cfr[i],
    quarantine = FALSE,
    vaccination = FALSE, 
    mass_culling = FALSE, 
    select_culling = FALSE,
    insecticide = FALSE
  )
  
  ## save full simulation to file
  out_file <- file.path(
    output_dir, 
    paste0(inputs$label[i], "_baseline", ".qs")
  )
  qsave(current_sim, out_file)
  
  ## store summary
  res_baseline[[i]] <- summary(current_sim)[duration, ]
}

res_baseline <- cbind(label = inputs$label, bind_rows(res_baseline))
head(res_baseline)
summary(res_baseline)

qsave(res_baseline, file.path(output_dir, "summary.qs"))
rio::export(res_baseline, file.path(output_dir, "summary.xlsx"))

cull_quarant_vacc scenario: mass culling and ring vaccination

In these simulations, we use:

  • mass culling of the affected herds
  • ring vaccination (i.e. vaccination of neighbours)
output_dir <- here::here("outputs", "simulations", "cull_quarant_vacc", Sys.Date())
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

res_cull_quarant_vacc <- vector(mode = "list", length = n_rep)
for (i in seq_len(n_rep)) {

  ## read a delta file for this simulation
  delta <- qread(delta_files[i])

  ## run simulation
  current_sim <- fast_lsdsim(
    grid_size = grid_size, 
    time = duration,
    ini_S = unlist(inputs$ini_S[[i]]), 
    ini_I = unlist(inputs$ini_I[[i]]),
    delta = delta,
    beta_A = inputs$beta_A[[i]],
    beta_I = inputs$beta_I[[i]],
    sigma = inputs$sigma[i],
    gamma = inputs$gamma[i],
    pasymp = inputs$pasymp[i],
    cfr = inputs$cfr[i],
    quarantine = TRUE,
    vaccination = TRUE, 
    mass_culling = TRUE, 
    select_culling = FALSE,
    insecticide = FALSE,
    interv_delay = inputs$interv_delay[i],
    quarant_efficacy_in = inputs$quarant_eff_in[i],
    quarant_efficacy_out = inputs$quarant_eff_out[i],
    interv_release = 28,
    vacc_coverage = 1, 
    vacc_efficacy = 0.65/21,
    rate_cull = 1e30
  )
  
  ## save full simulation to file
  out_file <- file.path(
    output_dir, 
    paste0(inputs$label[i], "_cull_quarant_vacc", ".qs")
  )
  qsave(current_sim, out_file)
  
  ## store summary
  res_cull_quarant_vacc[[i]] <- summary(current_sim)[duration, ]
}

res_cull_quarant_vacc <- cbind(label = inputs$label, bind_rows(res_cull_quarant_vacc))
head(res_cull_quarant_vacc)
summary(res_cull_quarant_vacc)

qsave(res_cull_quarant_vacc, file.path(output_dir, "summary.qs"))
rio::export(res_cull_quarant_vacc, file.path(output_dir, "summary.xlsx"))

quarant_vacc scenario: quarantine and ring vaccination

In these simulations, we use:

  • quarantine of infected herds and neighbours
  • vaccination of infected herds and neighbours
output_dir <- here::here("outputs", "simulations", "quarant_vacc", Sys.Date())
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

res_quarant_vacc <- vector(mode = "list", length = n_rep)
for (i in seq_len(n_rep)) {

  ## read a delta file for this simulation
  delta <- qread(delta_files[i])

  ## run simulation
  current_sim <- fast_lsdsim(
    grid_size = grid_size, 
    time = duration,
    ini_S = unlist(inputs$ini_S[[i]]), 
    ini_I = unlist(inputs$ini_I[[i]]),
    delta = delta,
    beta_A = inputs$beta_A[[i]],
    beta_I = inputs$beta_I[[i]],
    sigma = inputs$sigma[i],
    gamma = inputs$gamma[i],
    pasymp = inputs$pasymp[i],
    cfr = inputs$cfr[i],
    quarantine = TRUE,
    vaccination = TRUE, 
    mass_culling = FALSE, 
    select_culling = FALSE,
    insecticide = FALSE,
    interv_delay = inputs$interv_delay[i],
    quarant_efficacy_in = inputs$quarant_eff_in[i],
    quarant_efficacy_out = inputs$quarant_eff_out[i],
    interv_release = 28,
    vacc_coverage = 1, 
    vacc_efficacy = 0.65/21
  )
  
  ## save full simulation to file
  out_file <- file.path(
    output_dir, 
    paste0(inputs$label[i], "_quarant_vacc", ".qs")
  )
  qsave(current_sim, out_file)
  
  ## store summary
  res_quarant_vacc[[i]] <- summary(current_sim)[duration, ]
}

res_quarant_vacc <- cbind(label = inputs$label, bind_rows(res_quarant_vacc))
head(res_quarant_vacc)
summary(res_quarant_vacc)

qsave(res_quarant_vacc, file.path(output_dir, "summary.qs"))
rio::export(res_quarant_vacc, file.path(output_dir, "summary.xlsx"))

selcull_quarant_vacc scenario: selective culling, quarantine and ring vaccination

In these simulations, we use:

  • selective culling of infected individuals
  • quarantine of infected herds and neighbours
  • vaccination of infected herds and neighbours
output_dir <- here::here("outputs", "simulations", "selcull_quarant_vacc", Sys.Date())
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

res_selcull_quarant_vacc <- vector(mode = "list", length = n_rep)
for (i in seq_len(n_rep)) {
 
  ## read a delta file for this simulation
  delta <- qread(delta_files[i])

  ## run simulation
  current_sim <- fast_lsdsim(
    grid_size = grid_size, 
    time = duration,
    ini_S = unlist(inputs$ini_S[[i]]), 
    ini_I = unlist(inputs$ini_I[[i]]),
    delta = delta,
    beta_A = inputs$beta_A[[i]],
    beta_I = inputs$beta_I[[i]],
    sigma = inputs$sigma[i],
    gamma = inputs$gamma[i],
    pasymp = inputs$pasymp[i],
    cfr = inputs$cfr[i],
    quarantine = TRUE,
    vaccination = TRUE, 
    mass_culling = FALSE,
    select_culling = TRUE,
    insecticide = FALSE,
    interv_delay = inputs$interv_delay[i],
    quarant_efficacy_in = inputs$quarant_eff_in[i],
    quarant_efficacy_out = inputs$quarant_eff_out[i],
    interv_release = 28,
    vacc_efficacy = 0.65/21, 
    rate_cull = 1e30 # immediate culling
  )
  
  ## save full simulation to file
  out_file <- file.path(
    output_dir, 
    paste0(inputs$label[i], "_selcull_quarant_vacc", ".qs")
  )
  qsave(current_sim, out_file)
  
  ## store summary
  res_selcull_quarant_vacc [[i]] <- summary(current_sim)[duration, ]
}

res_selcull_quarant_vacc  <- cbind(
  label = inputs$label, 
  bind_rows(res_selcull_quarant_vacc )
)

head(res_selcull_quarant_vacc )
summary(res_selcull_quarant_vacc)

qsave(res_selcull_quarant_vacc, file.path(output_dir, "summary.qs"))
rio::export(res_selcull_quarant_vacc, file.path(output_dir, "summary.xlsx"))

selcull_quarant_vacc_insect scenario: selective culling, quarantine, ring vaccination, insecticide (all but mass culling)

In these simulations, we use:

  • selective culling of infected individuals
  • quarantine of infected herds and neighbours
  • vaccination of infected herds and neighbours
  • insecticide spray of infected herds and neighbours
output_dir <- here::here("outputs", "simulations", "selcull_quarant_vacc_insect", Sys.Date())
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

res_selcull_quarant_vacc_insect <- vector(mode = "list", length = n_rep)
for (i in seq_len(n_rep)) {

  ## read a delta file for this simulation
  delta <- qread(delta_files[i])

  ## run simulation
  current_sim <- fast_lsdsim(
    grid_size = grid_size, 
    time = duration,
    ini_S = unlist(inputs$ini_S[[i]]), 
    ini_I = unlist(inputs$ini_I[[i]]),
    delta = delta,
    beta_A = inputs$beta_A[[i]],
    beta_I = inputs$beta_I[[i]],
    sigma = inputs$sigma[i],
    gamma = inputs$gamma[i],
    pasymp = inputs$pasymp[i],
    cfr = inputs$cfr[i],
    quarantine = TRUE,
    vaccination = TRUE, 
    mass_culling = FALSE,
    select_culling = TRUE,
    insecticide = TRUE,
    interv_delay = inputs$interv_delay[i],
    quarant_efficacy_in = inputs$quarant_eff_in[i],
    quarant_efficacy_out = inputs$quarant_eff_out[i],
    insect_efficacy = inputs$insect_eff[i],
    interv_release = 28,
    vacc_coverage = 1, 
    vacc_efficacy = 0.65/21,
    rate_cull = 1e30
  )
  
  ## save full simulation to file
  out_file <- file.path(
    output_dir, 
    paste0(inputs$label[i], "_selcull_quarant_vacc_insect", ".qs")
  )
  qsave(current_sim, out_file)
  
  ## store summary
  res_selcull_quarant_vacc_insect[[i]] <- summary(current_sim)[duration, ]
}

res_selcull_quarant_vacc_insect <- cbind(label = inputs$label, bind_rows(res_selcull_quarant_vacc_insect))
head(res_selcull_quarant_vacc_insect)
summary(res_selcull_quarant_vacc_insect)

qsave(res_selcull_quarant_vacc_insect, file.path(output_dir, "summary.qs"))
rio::export(res_selcull_quarant_vacc_insect, file.path(output_dir, "summary.xlsx"))

all scenario: mass culling, quarantine, ring vaccination, insecticide

In these simulations, we use mass culling, ring quarantine and vaccination, and insecticide spray.

output_dir <- here::here("outputs", "simulations", "all", Sys.Date())
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

res_all <- vector(mode = "list", length = n_rep)
for (i in seq_len(n_rep)) {

  ## read a delta file for this simulation
  delta <- qread(delta_files[i])

  ## run simulation
  current_sim <- fast_lsdsim(
    grid_size = grid_size, 
    time = duration,
    ini_S = unlist(inputs$ini_S[[i]]), 
    ini_I = unlist(inputs$ini_I[[i]]),
    delta = delta,
    beta_A = inputs$beta_A[[i]],
    beta_I = inputs$beta_I[[i]],
    sigma = inputs$sigma[i],
    gamma = inputs$gamma[i],
    pasymp = inputs$pasymp[i],
    cfr = inputs$cfr[i],
    interv_delay = inputs$interv_delay[i],
    quarant_efficacy_in = inputs$quarant_eff_in[i],
    quarant_efficacy_out = inputs$quarant_eff_out[i],
    insect_efficacy = inputs$insect_eff[i],
    quarantine = TRUE,
    vaccination = TRUE, 
    mass_culling = TRUE, 
    select_culling = TRUE, # redundant with mass culling but for consistency
    insecticide = TRUE,
    interv_release = 28,
    vacc_coverage = 1, 
    vacc_efficacy = 0.65/21,
    rate_cull = 1e30
  )
  
  ## save full simulation to file
  out_file <- file.path(
    output_dir, 
    paste0(inputs$label[i], "_all", ".qs")
  )
  qsave(current_sim, out_file)
  
  ## store summary
  res_all[[i]] <- summary(current_sim)[duration, ]
}

res_all <- cbind(label = inputs$label, bind_rows(res_all))
head(res_all)
summary(res_all)

qsave(res_all, file.path(output_dir, "summary.qs"))
rio::export(res_all, file.path(output_dir, "summary.xlsx"))

Analyses

res_baseline$scenario <- "baseline"
res_cull_quarant_vacc$scenario <- "cull_quarant_vacc"
res_quarant_vacc$scenario <- "quarant_vacc"
res_selcull_quarant_vacc$scenario <- "selcull_quarant_vacc"
res_selcull_quarant_vacc_insect$scenario <- "selcull_quarant_vacc_insect"
res_all$scenario <- "all"

res <- bind_rows(
  res_baseline, 
  res_cull_quarant_vacc, 
  res_quarant_vacc, 
  res_selcull_quarant_vacc,
  res_selcull_quarant_vacc_insect,
  res_all
  ) %>% 
  select(scenario, everything()) %>% 
  mutate(
    p_immun = (R + V) / N,
    p_dead = (D + C) / (N + D + C), 
    scenario = factor(
      scenario, levels = c(
        "baseline", 
        "cull_quarant_vacc", 
        "quarant_vacc",
        "selcull_quarant_vacc",
        "selcull_quarant_vacc_insect",
        "all")
      )
  )

file_name <- paste0("res_", Sys.Date(), ".qs")
qsave(res, here::here("outputs", file_name))

This is evaluated only if new results have not been obtained, fetching the latest version of the results:

all_files <- list.files(here::here("outputs"), pattern = "^res")
extract_date <- function(x) {
  out <- gsub("[^[:digit:]-]", "", x)
  as.Date(out)
}
dates <- sapply(all_files, extract_date)
to_keep <- which.max(dates)
file_to_keep <- all_files[to_keep]
file_to_keep
## [1] "res_2026-05-09.qs"
message("reading results from file:", file_to_keep)
## reading results from file:res_2026-05-09.qs
res <- qread(here::here("outputs", file_to_keep))
label_numbers <- function(x, show_1_as_0 = FALSE) {
  out <- scales::label_number(scale_cut = scales::cut_long_scale())(x)
  if (show_1_as_0) {
    out[out=="1"] <- "0"
  }
  out
}

p_cases <- ggplot(res, aes(x = scenario, y = total_cases)) + 
  geom_violin(scale = "width", fill = "#F5275E", alpha = 0.65) + 
  scale_y_continuous(
    transform = "log10", 
    labels = function(e) label_numbers(e, show_1_as_0 = TRUE)
  ) + 
  theme_bw() + 
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = "Total cases"
  )

p_n_pop <- ggplot(res, aes(x = scenario, y = n_pop_ever_infec)) + 
  geom_violin(scale = "width", fill = "#F5275E", alpha = 0.65) + 
  scale_y_continuous(
    transform = "log10", 
    labels = function(e) label_numbers(e, show_1_as_0 = TRUE)
  ) + 
  theme_bw() + 
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = "Number of\nfarms affected"
    )


p_immun <- ggplot(res, aes(x = scenario, y = p_immun)) + 
  geom_violin(scale = "width", fill = "#F5275E", alpha = 0.65) + 
  theme_bw() + 
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = "Proportion of\nimmunized cattles"
    )

p_dead <- ggplot(res, aes(x = scenario, y = (D+C))) + 
  geom_violin(scale = "width", fill = "#F5275E", alpha = 0.65) + 
  scale_y_continuous(
    transform = "log10", 
    labels = function(e) label_numbers(e, show_1_as_0 = TRUE)
  ) + 
  theme_bw() + 
  labs(
    x = "",
    y = "Number of\ndead cattles"
  )

rotated_x <- theme(
  axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, vjust = 1, hjust=1)
  )

plot_grid(
  p_cases + rotated_x, 
  p_n_pop + rotated_x,
  p_immun + rotated_x, 
  p_dead + rotated_x,
  labels = LETTERS[1:4]
)
## Warning in scale_y_continuous(transform = "log10", labels = function(e)
## label_numbers(e, : log-10 transformation introduced infinite values.
## Warning: Removed 1105 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range
## (`stat_ydensity()`).

Some summary statistics:

# f_smry <- function(x) {
#   out <- c(
#     mean = mean(x), 
#     median = median(x), 
#     CI_95 = sprintf("[%f;%f]", 
#                     quantile(x, 0.025), 
#                     quantile(x, 0.975)
#                     )
#   )
# }

# total cases
res %>% 
  group_by(scenario) %>% 
  summarise(
    mean_cases = mean(total_cases) %>% label_numbers(), 
    median_cases = median(total_cases) %>% label_numbers(), 
    cases_0.025 = quantile(total_cases, 0.025) %>% label_numbers(),
    cases_0.975 = quantile(total_cases, 0.975) %>% label_numbers(),
    p_greater_100_cases = mean(total_cases >= 100) 
  ) %>% 
  knitr::kable(digits = 4)
scenario mean_cases median_cases cases_0.025 cases_0.975 p_greater_100_cases
baseline 9K 6K 1 31K 0.705
cull_quarant_vacc 3 2 1 13 0.000
quarant_vacc 56 17 1 452 0.113
selcull_quarant_vacc 52 12 1 350 0.147
selcull_quarant_vacc_insect 8 4 1 38 0.004
all 3 2 1 13 0.000 

# attack rates
res %>% 
  group_by(scenario) %>% 
  summarise(
    mean_ar = mean(ar), 
    median_ar = median(ar), 
    ar_0.025 = quantile(ar, 0.025),
    ar_0.975 = quantile(ar, 0.975)
  ) %>% 
  knitr::kable(digits = 4)
scenario mean_ar median_ar ar_0.025 ar_0.975
baseline 0.1070 0.0781 0 0.3891
cull_quarant_vacc 0.0000 0.0000 0 0.0002
quarant_vacc 0.0007 0.0002 0 0.0056
selcull_quarant_vacc 0.0007 0.0002 0 0.0044
selcull_quarant_vacc_insect 0.0001 0.0000 0 0.0005
all 0.0000 0.0000 0 0.0002 
  
# deaths
res %>% 
  group_by(scenario) %>% 
  summarise(
    mean_deaths = mean(C+D) %>% label_numbers(), 
    median_deaths = median(C+D) %>% label_numbers(), 
    deaths_0.025 = quantile(C+D, 0.025) %>% label_numbers(),
    deaths_0.975 = quantile(C+D, 0.975) %>% label_numbers()
  ) %>% 
  knitr::kable()
scenario mean_deaths median_deaths deaths_0.025 deaths_0.975
baseline 236 154 0 938
cull_quarant_vacc 73 58 23 222
quarant_vacc 2 0 0 14
selcull_quarant_vacc 48 11 0 320
selcull_quarant_vacc_insect 7 3 0 31
all 74 58 23 218 

# farms affected
res %>% 
  group_by(scenario) %>% 
  summarise(
    mean_farms = mean(n_pop_ever_infec),
    median_farms = median(n_pop_ever_infec),
    farms_0.025 = quantile(n_pop_ever_infec, 0.025),
    farms_0.975 = quantile(n_pop_ever_infec, 0.975)
    ) %>% 
  knitr::kable(digits = 2)
scenario mean_farms median_farms farms_0.025 farms_0.975
baseline 406.88 328.5 1 1312.03
cull_quarant_vacc 1.35 1.0 1 3.00
quarant_vacc 4.06 1.0 1 29.02
selcull_quarant_vacc 3.07 1.0 1 18.00
selcull_quarant_vacc_insect 1.45 1.0 1 4.02
all 1.36 1.0 1 3.00
